category_title: Young adults with intellectual disability who exhibit passive agreement and off-task behaviors during transitions
scenario_title: Developing self-monitoring intervention for transition lateness
minimal_context: Michael and his tutor are in a one-on-one meeting selecting a self-monitoring tool and setting goals for his transition to Social Studies.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to select a self-monitoring tool for your transitions. You can use a self-monitoring app on your phone or a paper chart.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am concerned about using my phone during classes. I want to use the paper chart.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Alright, we will use the paper chart. What is something you do right now that makes you late to Social Studies?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Going a way I like to go by the windows or something.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay. What should we set as your goal for the self-monitoring intervention?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I want to set a goal of being on time for all of my classes.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We need to select a self-monitoring tool for your transitions. You can use a self-monitoring app on your phone or a paper chart.

STUDENT: I am concerned about using my phone during classes. I want to use the paper chart.

TUTOR: Alright, we will use the paper chart. What is something you do right now that makes you late to Social Studies?

STUDENT: Going a way I like to go by the windows or something.

TUTOR: Okay. What should we set as your goal for the self-monitoring intervention?

STUDENT: I want to set a goal of being on time for all of my classes.
